mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment directly reduces aggregate demand and consumer confidence, depressing corporate earnings expectations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Sign-Rank IC between UNRATE and forward 20d returns is not negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10) in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below a low threshold (3%), it signals an accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and encourages risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: On days when FEDFUNDS < 3% holds, average forward 5d return is not significantly greater than unconditional mean (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and rising unemployment is a powerful recession signal that severely depresses forward-looking growth expectations and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, forward 60d returns are not significantly negative (one-sided test p < 0.05) or the joint predictive power does not exceed individual series effects (partial F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are elevated above a high threshold, a further increase reflects robust consumer spending that supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: When RSAFS > 375000, forward 5d returns are not significantly greater than unconditional mean (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) over the subset of days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which lifts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and forward 60d returns is not positive over any 2-year window, or the signal is not statistically significant in predictive regression (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumption and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: On months when PAYEMS increases, forward 20d returns are not significantly greater than on months when PAYEMS decreases (two-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of very low interest rates and collapsing housing starts traps the economy in a liquidity trap where accommodative policy fails to revive housing, leading to persistent negative equity returns over a short horizon as confidence continues to erode.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, forward 5d returns are not significantly negative (one-tailed test p < 0.05), or the interaction term in a regression is not significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, further increases in consumer prices erode real earnings and force the Fed to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as the market prices in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 210, forward 20d returns are not significantly negative (one-tailed test p < 0.10), or the predictive coefficient is not negative with marginal significance (t-test p > 0.10).
